skill.name: Azure AI Agents Persistent SDK for Java
skill.description: Low-level SDK for creating and managing AI agents with threads, messages, runs, and tools.
skill.summary: Azure AI Agents Persistent SDK documentation for Java developers building persistent AI agent workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines. Risk score: 0/100. This skill contains only documentation (SKILL.md) describing the Azure AI Agents Persistent SDK for Java. No executable code, scripts, or security-sensitive patterns detected. The documentation describes standard Azure authentication patterns using DefaultAzureCredential which is the recommended secure approach.

content.user_title: Create persistent AI agents with Azure SDK
content.value_statement: Build persistent AI agents that maintain state across sessions. This SDK provides low-level control over agent threads, messages, and runs in Java applications.
actual_capabilities: Create and configure persistent AI agents with custom instructions
Manage agent threads for multi-turn conversations
Execute agent runs with polling and status monitoring
Integrate custom tools with agent workflows
Clean up agent resources and threads
limitations: Requires Azure AI project and endpoint configuration
SDK is Java-specific and not available for other languages
Production use requires proper Azure credential configuration

use_cases.title: Customer Support Agent Integration
use_cases.description: Embed a persistent support agent in your Java backend that maintains conversation history across customer sessions.
use_cases.target_user: Java backend developers building customer-facing applications

use_cases.title: Educational Tutoring System
use_cases.description: Build a personalized tutoring agent that tracks student progress and adapts teaching style over multiple sessions.
use_cases.target_user: AI engineers developing educational technology platforms

use_cases.title: Enterprise Assistant Workflow
use_cases.description: Create reusable agent instances for common enterprise tasks like document processing or data analysis.
use_cases.target_user: Solutions architects designing AI-powered enterprise solutions

prompt_templates.title: Basic Agent Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a basic Azure AI persistent agent using Java. Show me how to set up authentication with DefaultAzureCredential, create an agent with custom instructions, and send a simple message.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner getting started with Azure AI Agents SDK

prompt_templates.title: Multi-turn Conversation
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to build a multi-turn conversation flow with Azure AI persistent agents. Include thread creation, adding messages, running the agent, polling for completion, and retrieving all messages from the thread.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer building a chatbot with conversation history

prompt_templates.title: Agent with Custom Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to create an Azure AI agent that uses custom tools. Show me how to define tools, attach them to an agent, handle the RequiresAction status, and submit tool outputs back to the run.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user integrating external APIs with agent workflows

prompt_templates.title: Production Agent Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete production-ready example of Azure AI persistent agents in Java. Include proper error handling for all run statuses, async client usage for high throughput, resource cleanup in finally blocks, and environment-based configuration.
prompt_templates.scenario: Senior developer implementing enterprise-grade agent system

output_examples.input: Create a math tutor agent that helps students with algebra
output: Agent 'Math Tutor' created successfully with ID: agent_abc123
Assistant: To solve 2x + 5 = 15, first subtract 5 from both sides...

output_examples.input: Execute code to solve a quadratic equation
output: Assistant: The solutions are x = 2 and x = 3

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for secure production authentication instead of hardcoded keys
Implement proper polling delays (500ms recommended) between run status checks to avoid rate limiting
Always clean up resources by deleting threads and agents when no longer needed
anti_patterns: Skipping polling between run status checks which causes missed state transitions
Hardcoding credentials instead of using environment variables and Azure managed identities
Not handling all run statuses (RequiresAction, Failed, Cancelled) leading to stuck workflows

faq.question: What is the Azure AI Agents Persistent SDK?
faq.answer: The Azure AI Agents Persistent SDK for Java provides a low-level API for creating and managing persistent AI agents. Unlike stateless APIs, persistent agents maintain threads and conversation history across multiple sessions, enabling complex multi-turn interactions.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Azure AI Agents?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which automatically handles authentication through environment variables, managed identities, or Azure CLI credentials. Set the PROJECT_ENDPOINT environment variable to your Azure AI project endpoint URL.

faq.question: What is the difference between an agent, thread, and run?
faq.answer: An agent is the configured AI instance with specific instructions and tools. A thread is the conversation container that holds messages. A run is the execution of the agent against a thread, processing messages and generating responses.

faq.question: Do persistent agents survive application restarts?
faq.answer: Yes, agents and threads are stored server-side in Azure. You can retrieve existing agents and threads by their IDs across application sessions, enabling true persistence of conversations and agent configurations.

faq.question: How do I handle tool calls with agents?
faq.answer: When an agent requires tool execution, the run status becomes RequiresAction. You must submit the tool outputs using submitToolOutputsToRun, then continue polling the run status until completion.

faq.question: Is there an async version of the SDK?
faq.answer: Yes, use PersistentAgentsAsyncClient instead of PersistentAgentsClient for non-blocking operations. The async client is recommended for high-throughput scenarios and better resource utilization.
